title: Suppress 'Tools, systems, and domains' from review UI and queue it for handoff removal
epicSource: f-review-ux · Phase 4 review UX improvements approved 2026-07-19
userStory: As a user reviewing my bootstrapped profile, I want the 'Tools, systems, and domains' section invisible on the review page and automatically included as a deletion instruction in the corrections handoff, so that this unhelpful section is removed from my profile without any manual action.

acceptanceCriteria.text: `PhaseReviewCorrect` never renders a section card for any section whose `selectRenderer` result is `'suppressed'`. For 'Tools, systems, and domains', `HEADING_OVERRIDE_MAP` (story 019) already maps this heading to `'suppressed'`; the render loop must guard against it (e.g. `if (renderer === 'suppressed') continue`). `grep -n '"Tools, systems\|Tools, systems' work-hq/src/routes/onboarding.tsx` returns at least one match confirming the heading string is referenced in the suppression path. After implementation, loading the review page with a profile containing the 'Tools, systems, and domains' section produces no DOM element whose visible text includes 'Tools, systems, and domains'.

`buildProfileCorrectionsMarkdown` unconditionally emits a section-level removal directive for 'Tools, systems, and domains' in every corrections handoff block — even when the user made no other edits. The directive follows the exact section-level removal convention defined in file-contracts §4.3 at the time of implementation: the implementer must read §4.3 before writing this line and emit the convention verbatim. Do not invent a format. `grep -n 'Tools, systems' work-hq/src/routes/onboarding.tsx` returns at least one match inside `buildProfileCorrectionsMarkdown`.

The `Ask` block in `buildProfileCorrectionsMarkdown` is updated to append, unconditionally and after the existing ask text, the exact sentence: `Remove the ## Tools, systems, and domains section from context/profile.md entirely.` This sentence must appear verbatim — the casing, punctuation, and `##` prefix are exact. `grep -n 'Tools, systems, and domains section' work-hq/src/routes/onboarding.tsx` returns at least one match inside the Ask string literal or the `buildProfileCorrectionsMarkdown` function.

The suppression is driven by heading string alone, not by section body content. `selectRenderer('Tools, systems, and domains', '')` returns `'suppressed'`; `selectRenderer('Tools, systems, and domains', '- some bullet')` also returns `'suppressed'`. Confirmed by `grep -n 'HEADING_OVERRIDE_MAP\|suppressed' work-hq/src/routes/onboarding.tsx` returning the heading key in the override map declaration — not in a body-parsing or body-length conditional.

`skills/os-helper/SKILL.md` gains a note in or immediately after the Signal C action block documenting the section-level removal directive: when the corrections handoff contains a section-level removal directive (following the file-contracts §4.3 convention, as defined by the architect before implementation), the assistant must delete the entire named section — heading line and all body content — from `context/profile.md`. The assistant does not edit or replace the section; it deletes it. The note is concise (under 8 lines). `grep -n 'section.*remov\|entire.*section\|heading.*delet' skills/os-helper/SKILL.md` returns at least one match.

technicalNotes: Depends on story 019: `selectRenderer` and `HEADING_OVERRIDE_MAP` must already map 'Tools, systems, and domains' → `'suppressed'`. The render-loop guard (AC1) requires no new code beyond ensuring `PhaseReviewCorrect` skips the renderer slot when `selectRenderer` returns `'suppressed'` — this guard is also the correct defence against future suppressed sections. The handoff change (AC2/AC3) is a targeted modification to `buildProfileCorrectionsMarkdown` (line ~657 per story 013 impl notes): (a) always append the section-level removal directive for 'Tools, systems, and domains' in the section block — read file-contracts §4.3 for the exact format before writing; (b) append the Ask sentence unconditionally after the existing ask lines. IMPORTANT: file-contracts §4.3 does not yet define the section-level removal convention as of story 023 authoring (2026-07-19) — the architect will update §4.3 before implementation. Do not begin AC2 until §4.3 carries the convention. The SKILL.md edit (AC5) is a targeted addition to `skills/os-helper/SKILL.md` after the Signal C block — model the style on story 018's edits (concise, self-contained, under 8 lines). Files touched: `work-hq/src/routes/onboarding.tsx`, `skills/os-helper/SKILL.md`.
outOfScope: KV-grid renderer (story 020). Item-list renderer (story 021). Taxonomy picker (story 022). Suppressing any section other than 'Tools, systems, and domains'. Changing the profile-corrections handoff header line (frozen, file-contracts §4.3). Changing AGENTS.md. Implementing the section-level removal convention itself in file-contracts §4.3 — that is the architect's responsibility.

verificationPlan.checks: Returns at least two matches — one in the HEADING_OVERRIDE_MAP / render dispatch (AC1/AC4) and one inside buildProfileCorrectionsMarkdown (AC2/AC3).

verificationPlan.command: grep -n 'Tools, systems, and domains section' work-hq/src/routes/onboarding.tsx
verificationPlan.checks: Returns at least one match — confirming the exact Ask sentence is present inside buildProfileCorrectionsMarkdown.
Returns at least one match — confirming the SKILL.md note about section-level deletion is present after the Signal C block.
